title: A History of the World in 100 Objects
excerpt: A groundbreaking history of humanity told through 100 selected objects from the British Museum, spanning two million years from Stone Age tools to modern credit cards.
keyIdeas: Objects offer a unique perspective on history that texts alone cannot provide
Physical artifacts connect us directly to the people who made and used them
A global history can be told through objects that transcend cultural and temporal boundaries
keyTakeaways: Objects reveal aspects of past lives that written records omit
Every object carries multiple stories — of its creation, use, and discovery
The selection spans 2 million years and all inhabited continents
The book originated as a BBC Radio 4 series, later adapted for print
Objects range from humble everyday items to great works of art
whoShouldRead: Anyone interested in world history told through a fresh lens
Readers who enjoy narrative nonfiction with strong visual elements
Teachers looking for engaging ways to present history to students
whoShouldSkip: Readers seeking traditional political or military history
Those who prefer deep coverage of specific periods to broad overview
People who want text-only history without visual illustration
